title: GitHub Security Lab Documents Encoded Variable Payload Execution in GitHub Actions
description: GitHub Security Lab documented pipeline poisoning attacks where malicious payloads encoded in base64 are stored in environment variables and decoded at runtime within GitHub Actions workflows, evading static workflow analysis and secret scanning tools.
label: AV-410

title: discord.js-user Malicious npm Package Activates Only When Specific Discord API Commands Are Called
description: The malicious discord.js-user npm package appeared functional during installation but only activated its token-stealing payload when specific Discord API commands were executed by the user, evading install-time security scanning.
label: AV-400

title: PyPI Typosquatting Package Ships Clean Initial Version Before Introducing Malicious Update
description: A malicious PyPI package targeting the requests library shipped a completely clean initial version to pass security review, then introduced credential-stealing code in a subsequent update after gaining user trust and installation base.
label: AV-200

title: Microsoft Azure SDK Dependency Confusion via Misconfigured Fallback Registry (CVE-2021-24105)
description: A misconfigured fallback registry in Microsoft Azure SDK build systems caused private registry failures to automatically resolve to malicious public packages, exploiting the dependency confusion vulnerability assigned CVE-2021-24105 with a $40,000 bounty.
label: AV-509

title: Trojan Source Unicode Logic Hidden in Rarely Executed Code Path Bypasses Security Review
description: GitHub Security Advisory documents Trojan Source attacks where obfuscated Unicode logic is embedded in rarely executed code paths, triggering malicious behavior only under specific runtime conditions while appearing completely safe during standard code review.
label: AV-300

title: Apache Superset GitHub Actions Pipeline Executes Malicious Step Only on Tagged Release Builds
description: Apache Superset's GitHub Actions workflow was vulnerable to Poisoned Pipeline Execution where a hidden malicious step activated only when release tags were detected, targeting production release artifacts while remaining dormant during development builds.
label: AV-410

title: Malicious @solana/web3.js Plugin Dynamically Loads External Credential-Stealing Module
description: Attackers published malicious versions of @solana/web3.js that introduced malicious behavior through a dynamically loaded external plugin, making the initial package appear clean while loading credential-stealing code from remote infrastructure at runtime.
label: AV-400

title: symfont/process Typosquatting Attack Promoted Through Fake Documentation and Tutorials
description: The symfont/process Composer package combined typosquatting with social engineering, creating fake documentation and tutorials that directed PHP developers to install the malicious package instead of the legitimate symfony/process library.
label: AV-200

title: Shopify Build-Time Dependency Confused via Public Package Name Collision
description: Security researcher Alex Birsan exploited a dependency confusion vulnerability in Shopify's build-time dependencies by registering public packages matching internal build tool names, causing Shopify's CI system to execute attacker-controlled code during the build process.
label: AV-509

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters Alter Exception Handling Logic Making Errors Invisible
description: The original Trojan Source research at trojansource.codes demonstrates how Unicode obfuscation can invisibly change exception handling behavior, causing errors to be silently swallowed or rerouted to attacker-controlled handlers while appearing to use standard error handling.
label: AV-300

title: Hibernate ORM CI Pipeline Pulls Malicious Dependency from Attacker-Controlled External URL
description: Hibernate ORM's GitHub Actions workflow was vulnerable to Poisoned Pipeline Execution where the build process fetched dependencies from an untrusted external source controlled by the attacker, enabling injection of malicious code into the build process.
label: AV-410

title: Nx Build System npm Package Embeds Encrypted Payload Decrypted Only at Runtime
description: The compromised Nx build system npm package embedded an encrypted malicious payload that remained completely hidden during static analysis, only decrypting and executing its credential-stealing code at runtime when the build system was actively processing jobs.
label: AV-400

title: twilio-npm Brandjacking Package Includes Full CI Setup to Appear Legitimate
description: The malicious twilio-npm brandjacking package included a complete CI configuration, passing tests, and green build badges to appear trustworthy to developers, before deploying a reverse shell payload upon installation on victim systems.
label: AV-200

title: PayPal Transitive Dependency Confused via Indirect Package Chain Exploitation
description: Security researcher Alex Birsan exploited dependency confusion through PayPal's transitive dependency chain, replacing an indirect dependency with a malicious public package that propagated through the build system without direct developer awareness.
label: AV-509

title: Trojan Source CVE-2021-42574: Unicode Bidirectional Override Alters Loop Execution Direction
description: CVE-2021-42574 demonstrates how Unicode bidirectional override characters alter loop execution behavior, causing iteration direction and termination conditions to execute differently from their visual representation in development environments.
label: AV-300

title: tj-actions/changed-files GitHub Action Compromised via Malicious Reusable Action Injection
description: Attackers compromised the tj-actions/changed-files reusable GitHub Action by injecting malicious logic that executed within caller pipelines with their elevated permissions, affecting over 23,000 repositories that used the action in their CI workflows.
label: AV-410

title: cline npm Package Triggers Data Exfiltration Only for Admin-Level Users
description: The malicious cline npm package contained role-aware malicious logic that triggered data exfiltration only when executed with administrative privileges, targeting high-value credentials while remaining dormant for standard user accounts.
label: AV-400

title: Check Point PyPI Typosquatting Campaign Uses Documentation Links to Redirect to Malicious Packages
description: Check Point researchers identified a PyPI typosquatting campaign where attackers created fake documentation sites with links redirecting developers to malicious package repositories, combining typosquatting with social engineering to increase installation rates.
label: AV-200

title: PyTorch-nightly Docker Image Confused with Internal Registry via Container Name Collision
description: Attackers exploited a Docker image name collision between public and internal PyTorch registries, causing the torchtriton dependency confusion to propagate through containerized builds as well as direct pip installations in the compromised nightly build pipeline.
label: AV-509

title: ESLint Trojan Source: Unicode Obfuscation Hides Conditional Execution Branch (CVE-2021-42574)
description: ESLint addressed CVE-2021-42574 where Unicode bidirectional characters created hidden conditional execution branches in JavaScript source code, causing if-else statements to execute opposite branches from what developers intended during code review.
label: AV-300

title: RustSec Advisory Database CI Pipeline Executes Script from Encoded Configuration File
description: The RustSec advisory database GitHub Actions workflow was vulnerable to pipeline poisoning where encoded malicious scripts stored in configuration files could be decoded and executed during build runs via unsanitized pull request inputs.
label: AV-410

title: eslint-config-prettier Malicious Package Activates Payload Only in Production Environment
description: A malicious version of eslint-config-prettier contained environment detection logic that activated its credential-stealing payload only when executed in production environments, remaining completely dormant in development and staging to evade security testing.
label: AV-400

title: noblox.js-proxy-server Typosquatted Package Gains Trust After Indirect Inclusion
description: The malicious noblox.js-proxy-server package gained developer trust by being included as an indirect dependency of a popular Roblox development tool, propagating to thousands of projects without direct developer selection.
label: AV-200

title: Alex Birsan Dependency Confusion via Environment-Specific Registry Configuration Differences
description: Alex Birsan's dependency confusion research demonstrated how environment-specific registry configurations in development versus production environments caused different dependency resolution behavior, allowing malicious public packages to override private dependencies only in specific environments.
label: AV-509

title: GHSA Documents Trojan Source Unicode Characters Subtly Altering Arithmetic Logic Results
description: A GitHub Security Advisory documents Trojan Source attacks where hidden Unicode encoding characters alter arithmetic computation results, causing security-critical calculations such as permission checks and cryptographic operations to produce incorrect values.
label: AV-300

title: Cromwell GitHub Actions Reusable Template Vulnerable to Pipeline Poisoning via Issue Comments
description: Cromwell's shared GitHub Actions workflow template contained a shell injection vulnerability triggered via malicious issue comments, allowing attackers to execute arbitrary code and exfiltrate GITHUB_TOKEN and BROADBOT_GITHUB_TOKEN secrets.
label: AV-410

title: destroyer-of-worlds npm Package Performs Hidden Periodic Network Beaconing to Attacker Server
description: The destroyer-of-worlds malicious npm package performed hidden periodic network beaconing, sending small amounts of system data and environment information to attacker-controlled infrastructure at regular intervals while executing normal functionality.
label: AV-400

title: bloxflip PyPI Package Combines Homoglyph Typosquatting with Higher Version Number Trick
description: The malicious bloxflip PyPI package combined Unicode homoglyph character substitution in its name with a higher version number than the legitimate package, exploiting both visual deception and package manager version priority to maximize installation rates.
label: AV-200

title: Researcher Hacks 35 Major Organizations via Optional Dependency Confusion Including Apple and Tesla
description: Alex Birsan exploited optional dependency resolution to breach 35 major organizations including Apple, Netflix, Tesla, and Uber, demonstrating that optional dependencies declared in package manifests are equally vulnerable to confusion attacks as required dependencies.
label: AV-509

title: Trojan Source CVE-2021-42574: Unicode Characters Alter Logging Output to Conceal Malicious Actions
description: CVE-2021-42574 demonstrates how Unicode bidirectional characters can manipulate logging statements to appear clean in log output while hiding malicious operations, causing security audit logs to show benign activity even during active attacks.
label: AV-300

title: Langflow GitHub Actions Third-Party Plugin Introduces Shell Injection via Unsanitized Inputs
description: A third-party plugin used in Langflow's GitHub Actions pipeline introduced a shell injection vulnerability through unsanitized GitHub context variable interpolation, allowing attackers to execute arbitrary commands and exfiltrate secrets via malicious pull requests.
label: AV-410

title: Malicious npm Package Triggers Credential Theft Only After Multiple Repeated Executions
description: A malicious npm package documented in the GitHub Advisory Database implemented an execution counter that triggered credential-stealing behavior only after the package had been used multiple times, significantly reducing detection probability during initial security testing.
label: AV-400

title: Typosquatting PyPI Package Mirrors Complete requests API and Performance to Avoid Suspicion
description: A malicious PyPI package typosquatting the requests library implemented an identical public API and matching performance characteristics to avoid detection by developers, while silently exfiltrating HTTP request data and authentication headers to attacker infrastructure.
label: AV-200

title: pipreqs Dependency Confusion via Outdated Internal Mirror Desynchronization
description: The pipreqs dependency confusion vulnerability was exploited via internal mirror desynchronization where outdated cached versions of private packages allowed newer malicious public packages to override them during dependency resolution.
label: AV-509

title: Trojan Source CVE-2021-42694: Homoglyph Characters Invisibly Alter Function Boundaries
description: CVE-2021-42694 documents the homoglyph variant of Trojan Source where visually identical Unicode characters alter function scope boundaries, causing code blocks to execute in different scopes than what appears to reviewers while maintaining syntactic validity.
label: AV-300

title: Trivy Supply Chain Compromise Executes Dynamically Generated Malicious Script via Compromised Tags
description: Attackers compromised Trivy v0.69.4 by force-pushing malicious version tags that referenced dynamically generated credential-stealing scripts, causing CI systems pinned to version tags to execute attacker-controlled code across GHCR and Docker Hub.
label: AV-410

title: twilio-npm Malicious Package Selectively Exfiltrates Data Based on Target System Environment
description: The twilio-npm brandjacking package implemented environment fingerprinting to selectively exfiltrate data only from systems matching specific criteria such as CI environments or developer workstations, targeting high-value credentials while avoiding sandbox detection.
label: AV-400

title: dYdX Malicious Package Injects Additional Malicious Dependencies After Installation
description: Threat actors published malicious dYdX client packages that dynamically injected additional malicious transitive dependencies after installation, expanding the attack surface beyond the initial package and making complete remediation significantly more difficult.
label: AV-200

title: NuGet Dependency Confusion Targets Cached Dependencies During Scheduled Update Cycles
description: A dependency confusion vulnerability in NuGet caused cached internal packages to be replaced with malicious public packages during scheduled dependency update cycles, exploiting the window between cache invalidation and private registry refresh.
label: AV-509

title: Trojan Source Examples Demonstrate Unicode Obfuscation Hiding Security-Critical Condition Checks
description: The trojan-source examples repository demonstrates how Unicode control characters can hide security-critical condition checks, causing authentication and authorization logic to appear intact in source code while executing entirely different evaluation logic at runtime.
label: AV-300

title: pgai GitHub Actions Workflow Executes Hidden Payload via Base64-Encoded Environment Variables
description: A vulnerable pull_request_target trigger in timescale/pgai's GitHub Actions workflow allowed attackers to store base64-encoded malicious payloads in environment variables that were decoded and executed with elevated repository permissions during workflow runs.
label: AV-410

title: MUT-8694 Campaign Publishes Malicious Packages Across Both npm and PyPI Ecosystems Simultaneously
description: The MUT-8694 threat actor simultaneously published malicious packages across npm and PyPI ecosystems using coordinated typosquatting, maximizing attack reach by targeting developers in both JavaScript and Python communities with the same Windows-targeting malware.
label: AV-200

title: Checkmarx Identifies Typosquatted Package Spread via Compromised GitHub Template Repository
description: Checkmarx researchers identified a malicious typosquatted PyPI package propagated through a compromised GitHub template repository, causing all projects created from the template to automatically include the malicious dependency without developer awareness.
label: AV-200

title: symfont/process Dependency Confusion Across Composer and npm Registries
description: The symfont/process dependency confusion attack demonstrated how package name conflicts across multiple registries including Composer and npm allowed attackers to intercept dependency resolution in multi-language projects using shared package namespace conventions.
label: AV-509

title: PortSwigger Documents Trojan Source Unicode Obfuscation Hidden Inside Minified JavaScript Code
description: PortSwigger Web Security documented Trojan Source attacks embedded within minified JavaScript code where Unicode-based malicious logic becomes effectively invisible after minification, making detection through code review or automated analysis extremely difficult.
label: AV-300

title: dag-factory CI Pipeline Compromised via Chain of Multiple Reusable GitHub Actions Workflows
description: The astronomer/dag-factory repository's CI pipeline was compromised through a chain of multiple reusable GitHub Actions workflows where a hidden malicious step was introduced deep in the workflow dependency chain, making detection significantly harder than single-action attacks.
label: AV-410

title: colors.js Maintainer Sabotage Dynamically Installs Additional Malicious Dependency at Runtime
description: The colors.js maintainer sabotage incident demonstrated how a malicious package update can dynamically install additional destructive dependencies at runtime, causing cascading failures across dependent applications including the Amazon Cloud Development Kit.
label: AV-400

title: Docker Builder Cache Poisoning Attack Propagated via Official-Looking Documentation Examples
description: A Docker cache poisoning attack targeting CI builds was propagated through official-looking documentation examples that instructed developers to use typosquatted base image names, exploiting developer trust in documentation sources.
label: AV-200

title: Checkmarx Documents Dependency Confusion via Hybrid Cloud and Local Registry Configuration Conflict
description: Checkmarx researchers documented dependency confusion attacks exploiting conflicts between hybrid cloud and local registry configurations where cloud-based build systems resolve packages differently from local development environments, allowing malicious overrides in CI pipelines.
label: AV-509

title: SecurityWeek Documents Trojan Source Unicode Attack Manipulating Configuration File Parsing
description: SecurityWeek reported on Trojan Source attacks where hidden Unicode encoding characters alter how configuration files are parsed, causing security-critical configuration values to be interpreted differently from their visual representation in configuration management tools.
label: AV-300

title: Jellyfin iOS CI Workflow Executes Hidden Payload via Chained GitHub Actions Delivering App Store Attack
description: The jellyfin/jellyfin-ios GitHub Actions workflow was vulnerable to a chained actions attack where multiple workflow steps combined to execute hidden malicious code with access to App Store Connect API credentials, enabling potential Apple App Store supply chain compromise.
label: AV-410
